2606.07311.title: CULTURESCORE: Evaluating Cultural Faithfulness in Video Generation Models
2606.07311.paper_summary: As video generation models like Veo 3.1 and LTX-2 advance, their ability to accurately represent diverse global cultures remains a critical yet understudied frontier. Current metrics, such as VideoScore, only measure visual quality but offer no mechanism for assessing cultural faithfulness. Consequently, a model that replaces a Namaste with a handshake receives the same score as one that generates the gesture correctly. We propose CultureScore, a compositional evaluation framework that decomposes cultural faithfulness into three granular dimensions: Identity (who is represented), Context (culturally localized background), and Behavior (normative gestures and interactions). We operationalize this framework through an evaluation suite spanning 10 countries, yielding 6,180 generated videos across three state-of-the-art models. Our evaluation reveals that no current model achieves culturally faithful video generation: the best-performing model reaches only 56.8\% overall CultureScore, with Behavior the most challenging dimension, which remains below 52\% across all models. Furthermore, human preference rankings align directionally with CultureScore but are inverted relative to VideoScore; the highest-scoring model on visual quality was ranked last by annotators, underscoring that cultural faithfulness is an essential criterion for equitable video generation.
2606.07311.paper_summary_zh: 隨著像 Veo 3.1 和 LTX-2 的視頻生成模型不斷進步，它們準確表現多元全球文化的能力仍然是一個關鍵但未被充分研究的前沿領域。當前的指標，如 VideoScore，僅測量視覺質量，但沒有提供評估文化忠實度的機制。因此，一個將 Namaste 替換為握手的模型與正確生成該手勢的模型獲得相同的分數。我們提出了 CultureScore，一個組合評估框架，將文化忠實度分解為三個細分維度：身份（誰被代表）、背景（文化本地化的背景）和行為（規範性手勢和互動）。我們通過一個涵蓋 10 個國家的評估套件來操作化這一框架，產生了 6,180 個視頻，涵蓋三個最先進的模型。我們的評估顯示，當前沒有模型能夠實現文化忠實的視頻生成：表現最佳的模型僅達到 56.8\% 的整體 CultureScore，而行為是最具挑戰性的維度，在所有模型中均低於 52\%。此外，人類偏好排名在方向上與 CultureScore 一致，但相對於 VideoScore 則是顛倒的；在視覺質量上得分最高的模型被標註者排在最後，這突顯了文化忠實度是公平視頻生成的重要標準。
2606.07311.author: Anku Rani et.al.
2606.07311.authors: Anku Rani, Wei Dai, Shravan Nayak, Pattie Maes, Mahdi M. Kalayeh, Paul Pu Liang
